piece.title: Skyline
piece.notes: An instrumental song about watching the city from above. F major, warm and open. Classic verse-chorus-bridge structure with clear repetition (hooks) and subtle variation (living melody). The chorus is the view—sparse, wide, unchanging. Verses explore the journey. Bridge breaks the pattern. Uses song form to create listener expectation and payoff.

verse_A.name: verse_A
verse_A.notes: The climb up. I-V-vi-IV is the classic warm progression. Lyrical melody with root-fifth bass gives forward motion. Whole-note harmony blooms gently.

verse_B.name: verse_B
verse_B.notes: Minor movement (vi-IV-I-V). Motif develops here. Walking bass adds momentum. Counterpoint enters underneath. This verse takes a different tonal path but lands back where verse_A wanted to go.

chorus.name: chorus
chorus.notes: The view. I holding for two bars (the vista), then V as breath, back to I. Sparse, wide, open. Pedal bass anchors. This is the hook—same every time. Listener should recognize it immediately on repetition 2.

chorus_final.name: chorus_final
chorus_final.notes: Final chorus. Same progression and melody as chorus (exact recognition). Root-only bass and fade_out arc signal the end. The view fades as daylight goes.

bridge.name: bridge
bridge.notes: The moment of realization. IV-I-IV-V breaks the verse/chorus pattern. Full density builds tension. Developed motif, walking bass, counterpoint rises. This is the 'pause where we see the city lights come on' moment.
